Feb 04, 2025

Pritzker\xe2\x80\x99s Postmortem, Chuck Todd Unplugged, Matt\xe2\x80\x99s Grammys Report

Welcome back to The Daily Courant, your afternoon compendium of Puck\xe2\x80\x99s best new reporting.

First up today, John Ourand reveals the secret culprit in the bizarre Luka Don\xc4\x8di\xc4\x87 \xe2\x80\x93 Anthony Davis trade heard around the world. Superstars on winning NBA teams are never exchanged midseason, and generational scorers like Luka are prized possessions. Chalk it up to changing media economics\xe2\x80\xa6

Plus, below the fold: Lauren Sherman offers an inside look at the Stella McCartney \xe2\x80\x93LVMH breakup. Peter Hamby gets Illinois Governor JB Pritzker on the horn to discuss how Democrats can find a winning narrative on the economy. And Scott Mendelson scrutinizes the success of DreamWorks\xe2\x80\x99 Dog Man during a peculiar lull in the animation market.


Meanwhile, on the pods: Tara Palmeri is joined by Democratic insider Alex Hoffman on Somebody\xe2\x80\x99s Gotta Win to unpack all the chatter surrounding the D.N.C. elections. On The Town , Matt Belloni and Lucas Shaw offer their candid assessment of the 2025 Grammys. On The Grill Room , Dylan Byers books former Meet the Press anchor Chuck Todd for an exit interview after nearly two decades at NBC. On Fashion People , Lauren and Harper\xe2\x80\x99s Bazaar \xe2\x80\x99s Lynette Nylander assess last night\xe2\x80\x99s Marc Jacobs show. And on The Powers That Be , Leigh Ann Caldwell joins Peter to break down R.F.K. Jr. and Tulsi Gabbard \xe2\x80\x99s confirmation battles.

SPORTS

John Ourand

The Secret Culprit in the Luka-A.D. Trade

Early Sunday morning, just hours after the Lakers edged out the Knicks at Madison Square Garden, ESPN\xe2\x80\x99s Shams Charania dropped a bombshell: The Lakers and Mavericks were swapping their franchise stars\xe2\x80\x94Anthony Davis for Luka Don\xc4\x8di\xc4\x87. Instantly, the NBA\xe2\x80\x99s overcaffeinated hype machine kicked into overdrive, and social media gadflies were trying in vain to figure out who knew what and when . But at the core of the frenzy lay a much bigger question: How did this happen? Superstars on winning teams never get traded midseason. And generational scorers like Luka\xe2\x80\x94especially in smaller NBA markets like Dallas, and on winning teams in particular\xe2\x80\x94are seen as untouchable assets. As it turns out, the answer might be surprisingly straightforward: media economics.

Read Now

FASHION

Lauren Sherman

Hella Stella

Six years ago, LVMH announced it was taking a 49 percent stake in Stella McCartney\xe2\x80\x99s business, following her breakup with Kering, which had owned 50 percent of the company she launched in 2001. Now, McCartney is buying back her business from the luxury conglomerate, and is once again a solo operator. Insiders suggest that, among other things, LVMH\xe2\x80\x99s decision to shed McCartney was a signal to other underperforming brands in its portfolio that a new era of fiscal discipline and scalability has dawned. As Europe\xe2\x80\x99s largest company, endowed with billions in shareholder capital, LVMH has a fiscal responsibility to offload middling businesses\xe2\x80\x94and Stella McCartney was certainly unprofitable. McCartney will need to prove herself once more, but has the market already moved on?

Read Now

WASHINGTON

Peter Hamby

The Pritzker Prize

In times like these, a political party\xe2\x80\x99s red-rimmed eyes often turn to the states, where ambitious governors like to brag about how their state is \xe2\x80\x9ca laboratory of invention\xe2\x80\x9d doing things differently\xe2\x80\x94and, of course, better \xe2\x80\x94than their colleagues in Washington. Illinois Governor JB Pritzker is one of them. After lying low, like many Democrats, during the first week of Trump II, Pritzker has since launched an all-out media blitz attacking the president for signing an executive order pausing federal spending and temporarily freezing Illinois\xe2\x80\x99 Medicaid portal. Pritzker has quickly emerged as one of Trump\xe2\x80\x99s loudest critics within the Democratic Party, and is positioning himself as an all-but-certain presidential candidate in 2028. In conversation with Peter, Pritzker critiques the Democrats\xe2\x80\x99 economic messaging, considers how his party should engage with the culture wars, and reframes the popularity of Trump\xe2\x80\x99s immigration crackdown.

Read Now

HOLLYWOOD

Scott Mendelson

Turbulent Times in Toon Town

Dog Man, DreamWorks Animation\xe2\x80\x99s $40 million adaptation of Dav Pilkey\xe2\x80\x99s mega-popular graphic novel series, nabbed a robust $36 million domestic this past weekend. Aside from being an unusually lucrative debut for an animated film in January, it\xe2\x80\x99s also the latest evidence that Universal\xe2\x80\x99s non-Illumination animation label can mint new franchises from popular, but not ubiquitous, material. As Scott observes, the theatrical industry needs animated films\xe2\x80\x94many of this decade\xe2\x80\x99s top performers were films that justified a family trip to the theater\xe2\x80\x94but these days, the paucity of animated films is practically inexplicable. To wit: After Dog Man , the next cartoon from a major studio\xe2\x80\x94Pixar\xe2\x80\x99s Elio \xe2\x80\x94won\xe2\x80\x99t arrive until June. So why aren\xe2\x80\x99t more entertainment players trying to capitalize on the market opportunity?

Read Now

WASHINGTON

Tara Palmeri

All the Tea From the D.N.C. Elections, With Party Insider Alex Hoffman

Tara Palmeri begins by discussing the latest moves from the Trump administration and the ripple effects they could send throughout the economy. Then, Democratic insider Alex Hoffman tells Tara about what he witnessed at this past weekend\xe2\x80\x99s D.N.C. elections, where the party selected Minnesota\xe2\x80\x99s Ken Martin to help the Democrats rise from the ashes over the next four years. The two discuss what Martin\xe2\x80\x99s election means for the party\xe2\x80\x99s future, how Kamala Harris fits into the party moving forward, the need for the Democrats to run an autopsy report on what went wrong in 2024, and more.

Listen Now

HOLLYWOOD

Matthew Belloni

Inside the 2025 Grammys: Beyonc\xc3\xa9\xe2\x80\x99s Win, Chappell\xe2\x80\x99s Speech, and Kendrick\xe2\x80\x99s Sweep

Matt is joined by Bloomberg\xe2\x80\x99s Lucas Shaw to recap an exciting and star-studded 2025 Grammy Awards, break down what it was like to be there in person, and nominate their biggest winners and losers from music\xe2\x80\x99s biggest night. They also talk about Chappell Roan\xe2\x80\x99s acceptance speech pleading for record labels to treat their artists better, Kendrick\xe2\x80\x99s dominant night, and Will Smith\xe2\x80\x99s return to the awards show stage. The guys finish the show with an update to the 2025 Box Office Draft.

Listen Now

MEDIA

Dylan Byers

Chuck Todd Unplugged

NBC News vet and former Meet the Press anchor Chuck Todd joins Dylan for a candid conversation about his departure from the network after nearly two decades. He reflects on the decline of media trust, the urgent need to revitalize local journalism, and how the internet has reshaped news consumption. Chuck also scrutinizes the nationalization of politics before switching gears to explore how podcasts might evolve into dynamic, multihour programming, \xc3\xa0 la ESPN\xe2\x80\x99s signature daytime shows.

Listen Now

FASHION

Lauren Sherman

New York Loves Marc Jacobs

Lauren and Harper\xe2\x80\x99s Bazaar executive digital director Lynette Nylander serve up instant reactions to Monday night\xe2\x80\x99s Marc Jacobs show at the New York Public Library. They also talk tariffs (or lack thereof), Dua Lipa\xe2\x80\x99s Chanel commercial (cue Fine Young Cannibals\xe2\x80\x99s \xe2\x80\x9cShe Drives Me Crazy\xe2\x80\x9d), and the end of Biologique Recherche PF50 1970. (R.I.P.) Lauren also explains the Vuori phenomenon to Lynette.

Listen Now

WASHINGTON

Peter Hamby
Leigh Ann Caldwell

Cabinet Confirmation Roulette

Leigh Ann Caldwell joins Peter to analyze the high-stakes confirmation battles of two of Trump\xe2\x80\x99s most contentious cabinet picks: Tulsi Gabbard for director of national intelligence and R.F.K. Jr. for secretary of Health and Human Services. Leigh Ann breaks down the key dynamics at play, the senators to watch as the processes unfold, and what could ultimately tip the scales in the nominees\xe2\x80\x99 favor\xe2\x80\x94or seal their fate.
